narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout
narratives.thesis: The demand for AI-specific hardware and infrastructure continues to surge, driven by hyperscalers and enterprises building out AI capabilities, leading to strong performance for chipmakers and related equipment providers.

narratives.name: GLP-1 Obesity Wave

Technology.thesis: The Technology sector remains broadly bullish, primarily driven by the relentless expansion of the AI capex cycle, which is fueling demand across semiconductors, hardware, and software infrastructure. While some software application names face AI disruption concerns, the overall sentiment is overwhelmingly positive due to strong hyperscaler spending and innovation.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — forces a hawkish Fed repricing, hurting growth multiples
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced AI chips — significant supply chain disruption

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: The Consumer Cyclical sector is mixed, with some areas benefiting from digital adoption and strategic M&A, while others face headwinds from shifting consumer demand, inflation, and platform risks. Residential construction is also showing signs of weakness.
watchFor: Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — impacts consumer discretionary spending
Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% YoY — indicates weakening consumer
Mortgage rates spike above 7.5% — further pressures housing market

Communication Services.thesis: The Communication Services sector is mixed, with some companies benefiting from strong content performance and strategic growth initiatives, while others navigate evolving digital advertising landscapes and competitive pressures.
watchFor: Digital advertising spending growth decelerates below 5% YoY
Streaming subscriber growth for major platforms turns negative
New regulatory actions impacting social media or content platforms

Industrials.thesis: The Industrials sector is mixed, with strong tailwinds from aerospace & defense spending and commercial space economy growth, but headwinds in some traditional machinery segments and a neutral outlook for integrated freight.
watchFor: Defense spending cuts announced by major governments
Global trade volumes decline significantly
Manufacturing PMI drops below 50 for two consecutive months

Healthcare.thesis: The Healthcare sector is mixed, with strong bullish momentum from GLP-1 drug innovators and strategic acquisitions in vaccine development, but some headwinds for medical device companies due to product recalls and analyst downgrades.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors emerge with superior efficacy/safety profiles
Increased regulatory scrutiny on drug pricing
Major medical device recall or safety concern

Financials.thesis: The Financials sector is showing bullish momentum, driven by strategic growth initiatives in wealth management, strong credit services performance, and positive analyst sentiment regarding interest rate outlooks. Asset management firms are also expanding their global reach.
watchFor: Fed signals sustained hawkish stance or further rate hikes
Significant increase in loan defaults or credit card delinquencies
New regulatory burdens impacting bank profitability

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The Consumer Defensive sector is mixed, with some companies demonstrating resilience in discount retail and momentum in consumer health, while others face headwinds from shifting consumer preferences and operational challenges.
watchFor: Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — impacts consumer purchasing power
Significant slowdown in consumer spending on essential goods
Unexpected supply chain disruptions for consumer staples

Utilities.thesis: The Utilities sector is showing bullish signs, particularly in independent power producers and renewable energy, driven by strategic upgrades and strong operational performance.
watchFor: Unexpected regulatory changes impacting renewable energy incentives
Significant increase in interest rates, raising cost of capital for projects
Major grid failures or infrastructure challenges

Basic Materials.thesis: The Basic Materials sector is currently neutral, with some companies navigating geopolitical shifts in rare earth elements and facing downward pressure despite strategic partnerships and positive analyst ratings.
watchFor: Significant shift in global trade policies impacting raw material supply
Major geopolitical event disrupting supply chains
Unexpected decline in industrial demand for key materials

Precious Metals.thesis: Precious metals are currently bullish, driven by ceasefire hopes and their traditional role as safe-haven assets amidst geopolitical uncertainty and inflation concerns, making them attractive for diversification.
watchFor: Significant de-escalation of global geopolitical tensions
Strong and sustained rally in risk assets (equities)
Real interest rates turn significantly positive
